'''Isaac Liu''' (born 26 April 1991) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|lf}} and {{rlp|pr}} forward for the [[Leigh Leopards]] in the [[Super league]] and [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] at international level.

He has previously played for the [[Sydney Roosters]] in the NRL and won back-to-back NRL premierships with that club in 2018 and 2019. He represented [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] earlier in his career at international level.

==Background== 
Liu was born in [[Auckland]], New Zealand, and is of [[Samoa]]n<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.samoaobserver.ws/category/sport/59636 |title=N.R.L. kicks off with Samoans making up over 16% of playing squads. |publisher=Samoa Observer |author=Thomas Airey |date=12 March 2020 |access-date=1 May 2022}}</ref> and [[Niue]]an heritage.{{fact|date=April 2022}}

He played his junior football for the [[Otahuhu Leopards]] before moving to Australia as a teen and attending Keebra Park State High School on the [[Gold Coast, Queensland]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.keebraparkrugbyleague.com.au/prog_grad.asp|title=Does Keerba Parks Footy Program Work? Here's Proof!|access-date=24 June 2014|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140531012925/http://www.keebraparkrugbyleague.com.au/prog_grad.asp|archive-date=31 May 2014}}</ref> He is of [[Samoans|Samoan]] and Niuean descent.{{fact|date=April 2022}}

In 2011, Liu signed with the [[Sydney Roosters]]. He played for the Roosters' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team in 2011, scoring 2 tries in 19 games.<ref name="rleague.com">[http://www.rleague.com//db/player/l/lui_isaac/index.php] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120618105729/http://www.rleague.com/|date=18 June 2012}}</ref>

==Playing career==
In round 4 of the [[2013 NRL season]] Liu made his [[NRL]] debut for the Roosters against the [[Parramatta Eels]] in a 50-0 victory at the [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|Sydney Football Stadium]].  Liu made 15 appearances for the club in his debut season including the 40-14 preliminary final victory over [[Newcastle Knights|Newcastle]] but he missed out on selection in the [[2013 NRL Grand Final]] side which defeated [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly-Warringah]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/sep/28/sydney-roosters-newcastle-knights-nrl/|title=Sydney Roosters surge past Knights and into grand final|website=The Guardian|date=28 September 2013}}</ref>
In May 2014, Liu played for [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] in the 2014 Pacific Rugby League International. He made headlines in the match after taking a massive shot by Fijian player [[Korbin Sims]].  Liu made 23 appearances for Easts in the [[2014 NRL season]] as the club won the minor premiership.  Liu played in the club's preliminary final defeat by [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]] which ended their premiership defence.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2014-09-26/rabbitohs-one-win-away-from-ending-title-drought/5773240/|title=South Sydney Rabbitohs beat Sydney Roosters 32-22 in NRL preliminary final at Sydney's Olympic stadium|newspaper=ABC News|date=26 September 2014}}</ref>
On 7 October 2014, Liu was selected in the Samoan 24 man squad for the 2014 Four Nations series.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/toa-samoa-name-four-nations-squad/tabid/10874/newsid/82434/default.aspx|title=Toa Samoa name Four Nations squad|work=NRL - The official site of the National Rugby League - NRL.com|date=7 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/toa-samoa-name-four-nations-squad-15250/ |title=Toa Samoa name Four Nations squad |work=NRL - Zero Tackle |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160203031215/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/toa-samoa-name-four-nations-squad-15250/ |archive-date=2016-02-03 }}</ref>
On 2 May 2015, Liu played for Samoa in the [[Polynesian Cup]] against Tonga.  In the [[2015 NRL season]], Liu made 26 appearances as the Sydney Roosters won their third consecutive minor premiership.  Liu played in the club's preliminary final loss against Brisbane at [[Suncorp Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/blog/live/2015/sep/25/nrl-preliminary-final-brisbane-broncos-v-sydney-roosters/|title=Brisbane win through to NRL grand final – as it happened|website=The Guardian|date=25 September 2015|last1=Connolly|first1=Paul}}</ref>

On 21 July 2017, Liu played his 100th First grade game against Newcastle, in this game he scored a try as the Roosters won the match 28-4 at the [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|Sydney Football Stadium]].  Liu made a total of 22 appearances for Easts in the [[2017 NRL season]] as the club finished 2nd on the table and reached the preliminary final before suffering a shock defeat by [[North Queensland Cowboys|North Queensland]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2017-09-23/nrl-preliminary-final-nq-cowboys-beat-sydney-roosters/8976278/|title=North Queensland Cowboys in another NRL grand final after beating Sydney Roosters 29-16 at SFS|newspaper=ABC News|date=23 September 2017}}</ref>
On 5 October 2017, Liu was named in New Zealand Rugby League world cup squad. Liu made 26 appearances for Easts in the [[2018 NRL season]] as they won the minor premiership and reached the [[2018 NRL Grand Final]] against Melbourne.  Liu played from the bench as Easts defeated Melbourne 21-6 claiming their 14th premiership.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2018/sep/30/nrl-grand-final-2018-sydney-roosters-v-melbourne-storm-live/|title=NRL grand final 2018: Sydney Roosters v Melbourne Storm - as it happened|website=The Guardian|date=30 September 2018|last1=Connolly|first1=Paul}}</ref>
Liu made 24 appearances for the [[Sydney Roosters]] in the [[2019 NRL season]] as the club reached the [[2019 NRL Grand Final]] against Canberra.  The club would go on to win 14-8 with Liu starting at prop.  It was the club's second consecutive premiership victory and Liu's second as a player.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49950757|title=Sydney Roosters beat Canberra Raiders to win NRL Grand Final|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>

Liu played 19 games for the [[Sydney Roosters]] in the [[2020 NRL season]].  The club reached the finals but were eliminated by Canberra at the [[Sydney Cricket Ground]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/10/09/raiders-march-on-as-three-peat-dream-ends-for-roosters/|title='More than a team': Ricky's resilient Raiders keep the dream alive|website=www.nrl.com|date=9 October 2020}}</ref>
On 7 July 2021, Liu signed a contract to join the [[Gold Coast Titans|Gold Coast]] for the start of the 2022 season.  Liu played 26 games for the [[Sydney Roosters]] in the [[2021 NRL season]] as the club reached the finals but were eliminated in the second week by [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/isaac-liu-gold-coast-titans-nrl-2021-sydney-roosters/1kafye213l982z988s59i7r08/|title=Isaac Liu joins Gold Coast Titans for 2022|website=www.sportingnews.com|date=7 July 2021 }}</ref>

=== 2022 ===
Liu played a total of 23 games for the Gold Coast in the [[2022 NRL season]] as the club finished 13th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-gold-coast-titans-season-review-justin-holbrook-tino-faasuamaleaui-david-fifita-toby-sexton-jayden-campbell-kieran-foran-jamal-fogarty/news-story/271373bd289c9c920124d2847fde6d5e/|title=Titans' million-dollar man goes missing as Holbrook rues costly mistake: Brutal Review|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=8 September 2022 }}</ref>

=== 2023 ===
Liu played a total of 21 matches for the Gold Coast in the [[2023 NRL season]] as the club finished 14th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-2023-gold-coast-titans-season-review/nel398u2vn7faognqvwnq7g4#:~:text=Did%20the%20Titans%20over%2Ddeliver,out%20of%20the%20finals%20picture./|title=NRL 2023: Gold Coast Titans season review|website=www.sportingnews.com|date=5 September 2023 }}</ref>

=== 2024 ===
Liu played a total of 23 matches for the Gold Coast in the 2024 season as the club again finished 14th on the table. On 9 September 2024, it was announced that Liu had been released by the club on compassionate grounds.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chalk |first=Ethan Lee |date=2024-09-09 |title=Gold Coast confirm departure of two-time premiership winner |url=https://www.zerotackle.com/gold-coast-confirm-departure-of-two-time-premiership-winner-214169/ |access-date=2024-09-09 |website=Zero Tackle |language=en-US}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=St John |first=Mark |date=2024-09-08 |title=Titans release forward on compassionate grounds as veteran farewelled — Off-season Central |url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2024-offseason-central-gold-coast-titans-farewell-two-players-erin-clark-compassionate-grounds-isaac-liu-contracts/news-story/caffcf0840a17ba42e7c21d72149f643 |access-date=2024-09-09 |website=Fox Sports |language=en}}</ref>

On 9 October 2024 it was announced that Liu had signed for [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] in the [[Super League]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=9 Oct 2024|author=Callum Walker|title=New Zealand international Isaac Liu makes Super League move|url=https://www.totalrl.com/new-zealand-international-isaac-liu-makes-super-league-move/}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Leopards sign Isaac Liu |url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/4642/leopards-sign-isaac-liu |access-date=2024-12-23 |website=www.superleague.co.uk}}</ref>

===2025===
Liu made his club debut for Leigh in round 1 of the 2025 Super League season against rivals [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]. Leigh would win the match 1-0 in extra-time after the game finished 0-0 at the conclusion of 80 minutes.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c2k5w971j35o|title=Leigh beat Wigan in extra time after historic 0-0 draw|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
Liu played 29 games for Leigh in the 2025 Super League season including their semi-final loss against Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/3-key-areas-that-saw-leigh-leopards-miss-out-on-old-trafford-as-grand-final-opportunity-passed-up/|title=3 key areas that saw Leigh Leopards miss out on Old Trafford as Grand Final opportunity passed up|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>
